doctor_description: No nodules requiring follow-up are identified. Lungs are unremarkable.
validator_description: Few micronodules on both sides (up to 2 mm).
notes: Validator identified bilateral micronodules up to 2 mm that the doctor did not mention. These are sub-threshold for Fleischner follow-up so no management change, but represent a real finding omission.

validator_description: Small subpleural areas of reticulation and ground glass in the right lower lobe may correspond to clinically insignificant interstitial changes.
notes: Validator noted subtle interstitial changes in the right lower lobe that the doctor missed entirely. Described as clinically insignificant, so no management change.

doctor_description: Free fluid in the basal pericardial recesses measuring up to 8 mm in axial thickness.
validator_description: Mild pericardial effusion.
notes: Both report pericardial effusion. Doctor provided precise measurement (8 mm), validator used qualitative descriptor (mild). Same clinical meaning.

doctor_description: Moderate coronary artery calcification.
validator_description: Moderate coronary artery calcification.
notes: Identical finding.

doctor_description: Mild calcification of the aortic and mitral valves.
validator_description: Mild calcification of the aortic and mitral valves.
notes: Identical finding.

doctor_description: Mild atherosclerotic calcification of the thoracic aorta.
validator_description: Mild calcification of the thoracic aorta.
notes: Doctor specified 'atherosclerotic,' validator omitted the qualifier. Same clinical meaning.

doctor_description: Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine, consistent with spondylosis.
validator_description: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.
notes: Doctor graded as mild, validator graded as moderate. Minor severity grading difference; no management change.

doctor_description: Hiatal hernia. Hernia sac measures up to 5.8 × 4.7 cm in the axial plane.
validator_description: Hiatal hernia.
notes: Both report hiatal hernia. Doctor provided detailed measurement; validator did not. Validator moved this from upper abdomen to mediastinum section. Same clinical meaning.

doctor_description: No lymphadenopathy.
validator_description: No lymphadenopathy.
notes: Concordant negative finding.

doctor_description: No pleural effusion.
validator_description: No pleural effusion.
notes: Concordant negative finding.

doctor_description: Subpleural irregular area of consolidation with air bronchograms measuring 2.6 × 2.0 × 2.5 cm with relatively well-defined irregular margins, bounded by interlobar pleura, with surrounding ground-glass opacity.
validator_description: Subpleural irregular area of consolidation with air bronchograms measuring 2.7 × 2.0 × 2.5 cm. In the consolidation area there are a single calcification up to 2 mm and bronchiectasis. On the periphery bronchocele, tree-in-bud nodules, ground glass areas.
notes: Validator added important morphologic details within the consolidation: calcification, bronchiectasis, bronchocele, and tree-in-bud nodules on the periphery. These details support the NTM infection differential. No direct management change but clinically relevant characterization.

doctor_description: Subpleural area of consolidation measuring up to 2 mm in the sagittal plane, with surrounding ground-glass opacity.
validator_description: Subpleural area of consolidation measuring up to 1.2 cm, with surrounding ground-glass opacity.
notes: Size discrepancy: doctor reported 2 mm, validator reported 1.2 cm. Likely a measurement or typographical error by the doctor (2 mm vs 12 mm). Same finding identified.

doctor_description: Mucus plugs in RUL segment 2, centrilobular ground-glass focus, intralobular Y-shaped opacities. Small peribronchial GGO in LLL segments 8-9 and RLL segment 10. Tree-in-bud in LLL segment 10.
validator_description: Polysegmental confluent centrilobular nodules (tree-in-bud) with areas of ground glass at the periphery. Secretion in subsegmental bronchi.
notes: Both describe tree-in-bud pattern and bronchiolitis. Doctor was more segmentally specific; validator used a more generalized bilateral description. Same clinical meaning.

validator_description: Apical caps.
notes: Validator noted apical caps not mentioned by the doctor. These are usually benign age-related findings with no management impact.

doctor_description: No clinically significant pericardial effusion.
validator_description: Mild pericardial effusion.
notes: Doctor called it not clinically significant; validator characterized it as mild pericardial effusion and linked it to inflammatory process. Minor clinical difference but no direct management change.

validator_description: Osteoblastic lesions in the bodies of the T5 and T9 vertebrae 1.1 cm and 1.0 cm respectively.
notes: Doctor completely missed two osteoblastic vertebral lesions measuring over 1 cm. In the setting of an inflammatory/infectious process with consolidation and tree-in-bud, these could represent metastatic disease, bone islands, or infection-related changes. This is a significant omission that may warrant further workup (e.g., comparison with prior imaging, possible bone scan or MRI).

validator_description: An ovoid lesion in the left breast 1.1 × 1.0 cm. Given the breast density, ultrasound is recommended.
notes: Doctor completely missed a 1.1 cm breast lesion that the validator flagged with a recommendation for ultrasound evaluation. This is a significant underreport as it directly changes management — breast ultrasound is now recommended.

doctor_description: Bilateral multisegmental pneumonia, CT severity score 1, total involved lung volume up to 5%. Infectious bronchiolitis in LLL segment 10.
validator_description: Bilateral bronchiolitis, bilateral subpleural areas of consolidation with bronchiectasis consistent with infectious/inflammatory process, including non-tuberculous mycobacterial (NTM) infection.
notes: Doctor diagnosed generic pneumonia; validator refined the differential to include NTM infection given bronchiectasis, tree-in-bud, and consolidation pattern. The NTM differential is clinically more specific and may guide workup differently, but the immediate management (clinical correlation) remains similar.

doctor_description: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.
validator_description: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.
validator_description: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.
notes: Concordant negative finding.

doctor_description: No coronary artery calcification.
validator_description: No coronary artery calcification.
notes: Concordant negative finding.

doctor_description: No pulmonary nodules requiring follow-up according to Fleischner Society recommendations.
validator_description: No nodules requiring follow-up.
notes: Concordant negative finding.

doctor_description: Round lesion in left thyroid lobe with smooth, well-defined margins, up to 1.1 cm, with fluid attenuation. Cystic lesion, no follow-up required per ACR 2014.
validator_description: Round hypodense lesion in left thyroid lobe with smooth, well-defined margins, up to 1.1 cm. Per ACR White Paper 2014, thyroid ultrasound is recommended.
notes: Doctor characterized the lesion as cystic (fluid attenuation) and stated no follow-up needed. Validator described it as hypodense (not definitively cystic) and recommended thyroid ultrasound per ACR guidelines. The ACR White Paper recommends ultrasound for incidental thyroid nodules >1 cm on CT, so the validator's recommendation is more guideline-compliant. However, if truly cystic, the doctor's interpretation could also be defensible.

finding_category: Metallic foreign body / soft tissue assessment
anatomical_location: Left anterior abdominal wall / chest wall
doctor_description: A small metallic foreign body is identified in the skin of the anterior left abdominal flank.
validator_description: In the area of the external metal body on the left, no changes in the soft tissues, subcutaneous fat, or ribs are detected.
notes: Both noted the metallic foreign body. Validator added a clinically useful negative statement that there are no associated soft tissue, fat, or rib changes in the area — important for excluding penetrating injury sequelae. Doctor simply noted its presence without assessing surrounding structures.

doctor_description: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.
validator_description: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.
notes: Concordant negative finding.

doctor_description: No clinically significant pericardial effusion.
validator_description: No clinically significant pericardial effusion.
notes: Concordant negative finding.

doctor_description: No coronary artery calcification.
validator_description: No coronary artery calcification.
notes: Concordant negative finding.

doctor_description: Mild centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema in the apices.
validator_description: Mild centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema in the apices.
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: A single thin-walled subpleural bulla in the right upper lobe, segment 1, measuring 2.2 × 1.5 cm.
notes: Doctor specifically called out a subpleural bulla with measurements. Validator did not mention it separately, likely considering it part of the emphysematous changes. No management impact as bullae in the setting of emphysema do not require separate follow-up unless symptomatic.

doctor_description: In the apical portion of the left upper lobe, segments 1 and 2, a calcified granuloma measuring 4 × 3 mm.
validator_description: Calcified granulomas measuring up to 3 mm of both lungs.
notes: Doctor identified a single calcified granuloma in the LUL. Validator described calcified granulomas bilaterally. Both recognize granulomatous disease. Same clinical meaning.

doctor_description: Linear atelectasis in the left upper lobe segment 5, right middle lobe, and lingular and basal segments of the left lung.
validator_description: Linear atelectasis in the left upper lobe segment 5, right middle lobe, and lingular and basal segments of the left lung.
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: Membranous portion of the trachea prolapses into the lumen (study on incomplete inspiration). Small amount of adherent intraluminal content within the trachea.
validator_description: Membranous portion of the trachea prolapses into the lumen (study on incomplete inspiration). Small amount of secretion within the trachea.
notes: Doctor used 'adherent intraluminal content,' validator used 'secretion.' Same finding.

doctor_description: Mild coronary artery calcification.
validator_description: Mild coronary artery calcification.
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: Atherosclerotic calcification of the wall.
validator_description: Mild wall calcification.
notes: Same finding, slightly different wording.

doctor_description: Decreased height and wedge deformity of the T11 vertebral body up to 25 percent (Genant grade 1).
notes: Doctor reported a T11 compression fracture with Genant grading. Validator did not mention this and downgraded spine findings to 'moderate degenerative changes.' This may reflect the validator's judgment that the finding is chronic/degenerative rather than a true compression fracture, or it may be an omission by the validator. Given the clinical context, the doctor's more detailed description is arguably better practice.

doctor_description: Severe degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Diffuse calcification of the anterior longitudinal ligament. Ankylosis of the right seventh costovertebral joint.
validator_description: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Diffuse calcification of the anterior longitudinal ligament. Ankylosis of the right seventh costovertebral joint.
notes: Doctor graded as severe, validator as moderate. Otherwise identical details including DISH and ankylosis.

doctor_description: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.
validator_description: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.
notes: Concordant negative finding.

doctor_description: No nodules requiring follow-up.
validator_description: No nodules requiring follow-up.
notes: Concordant negative finding.

doctor_description: Mosaic attenuation pattern in the basal lungs. Impression: terminal bronchiolar obstruction consistent with chronic bronchitis versus bronchial asthma.
notes: Doctor reported mosaic attenuation and suggested small airways disease. Validator attributed the findings to incomplete inspiration and did not report mosaic attenuation. On an incomplete inspiration study, apparent mosaic attenuation can be artifactual. The validator's more conservative interpretation is reasonable.

doctor_description: Thin linear atelectasis in segment 8 of the left lower lobe.
validator_description: Incomplete inspiration, lungs not fully expanded, small discoid atelectasis.
notes: Both note atelectasis. Validator explicitly noted incomplete inspiration as the context. Same clinical meaning.

validator_description: Single cyst in the left upper lobe.
notes: Validator identified a cyst in the left upper lobe not mentioned by the doctor. A solitary cyst typically does not change management but should be documented.

doctor_description: Cardiothoracic ratio is 59%. Moderate cardiomegaly.
validator_description: Cardiothoracic ratio is 0.59, left ventricular enlargement.
notes: Both report cardiomegaly with CTR 0.59. Validator added specificity by identifying left ventricular enlargement as the chamber involved. Clinically useful detail but does not change management on a non-cardiac CT.

validator_description: Small hiatal hernia.
notes: Doctor missed a small hiatal hernia that the validator identified. No management change.

doctor_description: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine, including spondylosis and facet arthropathy.
validator_description: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine (spondylosis, spondyloarthrosis).
notes: Same finding, minor wording difference (facet arthropathy vs spondyloarthrosis are synonymous).

validator_description: Partial ossification of costal cartilages, bone tissue of the ribs is unremarkable.
notes: Validator mentioned costal cartilage ossification as a normal age-related finding. Purely descriptive, no clinical significance.

doctor_description: No mass or lymphadenopathy.
validator_description: No mass or lymphadenopathy.
notes: Concordant negative finding.

doctor_description: Irregular consolidation at the junction of segments 7 and 8, measuring up to 1.9 cm (previously 2.8 cm). Sharp spiculated margins, with surrounding tree-in-bud opacities.
validator_description: Mass-like consolidation with spiculated margins in the right lower lobe (1.9 cm, previously 2.8 cm), on the periphery multiple tree-in-bud nodules.
notes: Both describe the same consolidation with concordant size, margins, and associated tree-in-bud. Validator used 'mass-like' descriptor; doctor used 'irregular.' Same clinical meaning.

doctor_description: Previously described multiple pulmonary nodules are no longer seen.
validator_description: Numerous solid and mixed solid/ground glass nodules are no longer seen.
notes: Validator provided more specific characterization of resolved nodules (solid and mixed). Same clinical meaning — interval resolution.

doctor_description: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.
validator_description: No mass. A decrease in the size of lymph nodes compared with December 16, 2025, maximum size along the short axis up to 10 mm.
notes: Doctor stated no lymphadenopathy. Validator noted lymph nodes up to 10 mm short axis with interval decrease. A 10 mm node is at the upper limit of normal and the interval improvement is clinically relevant context for a patient being followed for an infectious process (likely coccidiomycosis). No management change but important for trending.

doctor_description: Impression limited to description of decreasing consolidation and resolved nodules without specific etiology.
validator_description: Persisting multiple tree-in-bud nodules probably consistent with coccidiomycosis.
notes: Validator provided a specific likely diagnosis (coccidiomycosis) given the clinical context (Arizona, resolving nodules and consolidation). Doctor did not offer a differential. This is clinically helpful but does not change immediate management.

doctor_description: Subcapsular hypodense lesion, unchanged in size, up to 1.3 cm, well-defined smooth margins.
validator_description: Subcapsular hypodense lesion, unchanged in size, up to 1.3 cm, well-defined smooth margins.
notes: Concordant finding. Identical description.

doctor_description: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.
validator_description: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: No coronary artery calcification.
validator_description: No coronary artery calcification.
notes: Concordant negative finding.

doctor_description: Areas of heterogeneous ground-glass opacity with irregular margins in the basal lungs, greater on the right. Heterogeneous aeration.
validator_description: Areas of ground-glass opacity with air trapping in the basal lungs.
notes: Both describe GGO in the basal lungs. Validator used 'air trapping' while doctor described 'heterogeneous' pattern. Same clinical meaning. Both suggest sarcoidosis in the impression.

doctor_description: A calcified focus in segment 9 of the right lung measures 4 × 2 mm.
validator_description: A 4 mm calcified nodule in segment 9 of the right lung.
notes: Same finding, minor wording difference. Validator added 'healed granuloma' interpretation in impression.

doctor_description: Scattered small nodules in segment 6 bilaterally and segment 5 on the left, measuring less than 1-2 mm.
validator_description: Few micronodules on both sides.
notes: Doctor was more segmentally specific; validator summarized. Same finding.

doctor_description: Linear atelectasis in segment 5 bilaterally.
validator_description: Linear atelectasis bilaterally.
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: Right bronchopulmonary lymph nodes up to 1.1 cm, subcarinal up to 1.3 cm, aortopulmonary up to 1.3 cm, right pulmonary ligament up to 1.2 cm.
validator_description: Bronchopulmonary lymph nodes up to 1.1 cm, subcarinal up to 1.3 cm, subaortic and paraaortic lymph nodes up to 1.3 cm.
notes: Both describe borderline enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes with similar measurements. Doctor included right pulmonary ligament nodes; validator omitted that station. Minor difference.

validator_description: Small tracheal diverticulum.
notes: Validator identified a small tracheal diverticulum not noted by the doctor. This is typically an incidental finding of no clinical significance.

doctor_description: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The pulmonary artery is not dilated.
validator_description: Thoracic aorta normal in caliber with mild wall calcifications.
notes: Doctor did not mention aortic calcification. Validator noted mild wall calcifications. Minor finding, no management change.

doctor_description: Severe degenerative changes of the thoracic spine, including osteochondrosis and spondylosis.
validator_description: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.
notes: Doctor graded as severe, validator as moderate. Minor severity grading difference.

doctor_description: Findings may be consistent with a granulomatous process, such as sarcoidosis.
validator_description: Mediastinal lymphadenopathy, bilateral GGO with air trapping may be consistent with sarcoidosis.
notes: Both suggest sarcoidosis as the primary differential. Concordant clinical assessment.

validator_description: Few micronodules and a healed granuloma; no further CT follow-up required per Fleischner.
notes: Validator explicitly stated Fleischner non-applicability for the micronodules. Doctor did not address follow-up recommendations. Stylistic/completeness difference.

doctor_description: Subpleural linear parenchymal bands in segments 9 and 10. Previously seen tree-in-bud opacities in segment 9 not present on this examination.
validator_description: Previously seen tree-in-bud opacities in the peripheral right lower lobe with surrounding ground glass partially resolved, residual reticulations.
notes: Both describe interval regression of inflammatory changes. Validator noted residual reticulations and partial resolution; doctor noted parenchymal bands and complete resolution of tree-in-bud. Slightly different characterization of the residual findings.

doctor_description: Solid polygonal perifissural nodule measuring 3 × 1 mm along the interlobar pleura (series 3, image 138). Impression: consistent with intrapulmonary lymph node, no follow-up required.
validator_description: No nodules requiring follow-up.
notes: Doctor specifically described a 3 mm perifissural nodule and correctly identified it as a likely intrapulmonary lymph node not requiring follow-up. Validator simply stated no nodules requiring follow-up. Both reach the same clinical conclusion — no follow-up needed. Doctor was more thorough in documentation.

validator_description: A small amount of secretion in the segmental and subsegmental bronchi.
notes: Validator noted bronchial secretions not mentioned by the doctor. Minor finding, consistent with resolving infection.

doctor_description: Moderate coronary artery calcification.
validator_description: Moderate coronary artery calcification.
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: Aortic valve calcification.
notes: Doctor mentioned aortic valve calcification; validator did not include this. Minor incidental finding omitted by validator.

doctor_description: Wall calcification is present.
validator_description: Mild wall calcifications.
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: Decreased height and wedge deformity of T7 by 28% and T11 by up to 27%. Chronic compression fractures.
validator_description: Decreased height and wedge deformity of T7 by 28% and T11 by up to 27%. Wedge deformity of T7 and T11.
notes: Concordant finding with identical measurements. Doctor labeled as 'chronic compression fractures,' validator used 'wedge deformity.' Same clinical meaning.

doctor_description: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine, severe in the lower thoracic spine.
validator_description: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.
notes: Doctor specified severity gradient (severe in lower thoracic); validator used a general statement. Minor detail difference.

doctor_description: Mild osteoarthritis of the shoulder joints within the field of view.
validator_description: Mild osteoarthritis of the shoulder joints within the field of view.
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: Mild thickening of the medial crus of the left adrenal gland.
validator_description: Benign lesions of both adrenal glands (adenomas), no additional workup or follow-up imaging is needed.
notes: Doctor noted left adrenal thickening only. Validator characterized bilateral adrenal lesions as adenomas and explicitly stated no follow-up needed. The validator provided a more complete assessment with a definitive benign characterization.

doctor_description: No mass or lymphadenopathy.
validator_description: No mass or lymphadenopathy.
notes: Concordant negative finding.

doctor_description: The main pulmonary artery is not dilated (26 mm).
validator_description: The main pulmonary artery is not dilated (26 mm).
notes: Concordant finding with identical measurement.

doctor_description: Previously seen pulmonary nodules are not identified.
validator_description: Previously seen pulmonary nodules are not identified.
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: No mass. Visible mediastinal lymph nodes are not enlarged.
validator_description: No mediastinal mass or lymphadenopathy.
notes: Same clinical meaning. Validator added 'improved compared with December 19, 2024' in the impression.

doctor_description: Doctor impression did not mention interval improvement for lymph nodes.
validator_description: Improved compared with December 19, 2024 (for both nodules and lymph nodes).
notes: Validator explicitly stated interval improvement for lymph nodes compared to prior study. Doctor noted improvement for nodules but not lymph nodes. Stylistic completeness difference.

doctor_description: A catheter is in place in the right subclavian vein.
validator_description: A catheter is in place in the right subclavian vein.
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: Moderate coronary artery atherosclerotic calcification.
validator_description: Moderate coronary artery calcification.
notes: Doctor specified 'atherosclerotic'; validator omitted the qualifier. Same finding.

doctor_description: Mild atherosclerotic calcification.
validator_description: Mild calcification.
notes: Same finding, minor wording.

doctor_description: Mild degenerative changes with mild scoliotic deformity of the thoracic spine.
validator_description: Mild degenerative changes and mild scoliosis of the thoracic spine.
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: Cholecystectomy.
validator_description: Cholecystectomy.
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: No pleural effusion.
validator_description: No pleural effusion.
notes: Concordant negative finding.

doctor_description: Numerous centrilobular and paraseptal emphysematous foci predominantly in the apical and mid lung regions.
validator_description: Moderate centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema.
notes: Both describe mixed emphysema. Doctor was more descriptive; validator used standard grading. Same clinical meaning.

doctor_description: Areas of decreased aeration with heterogeneous GGO and mosaic attenuation. Interlobular septa thickened in the basal lungs.
validator_description: Areas of GGO and mosaic attenuation. Interlobular septal thickening in the basal regions, subpleural reticular changes.
notes: Both describe the same pattern. Validator added 'subpleural reticular changes.' Minor descriptive addition.

doctor_description: Solid nodule measuring 6 × 7 mm along the interlobar pleura (series 3, image 129).
validator_description: Solid nodule measuring 8 mm (series 3, image 129).
notes: Same nodule, slightly different measurement (6-7 mm vs 8 mm). Within measurement variability.

doctor_description: Subpleural solid nodule measuring 7 × 6 mm (series 3, image 159).
validator_description: Subpleural solid nodule measuring 6 mm (series 3, image 159).
notes: Same nodule with concordant measurement.

doctor_description: Solid nodule with irregular, spiculated margins measuring 6 × 6 mm (series 3, image 155).
validator_description: Solid nodule with irregular, spiculated margins measuring 6 mm (series 3, image 155).
notes: Concordant finding with identical characterization. This spiculated nodule is the most concerning of the three.

validator_description: Multiple micronodules on both sides.
notes: Validator identified multiple bilateral micronodules not mentioned by the doctor. In the context of known larger nodules and possible hypersensitivity pneumonitis/cardiac disease, these are worth documenting.

doctor_description: Polymorphic parenchymal bands in segments 8 and 9 of the right lung, likely linear atelectasis.
notes: Doctor mentioned parenchymal bands/atelectasis not separately noted by validator. Minor finding with no clinical impact.

doctor_description: Small amount of free fluid in the left pleural space up to 4 mm. Mild amount in the right interlobar fissure.
validator_description: Small amount of free fluid in the pleural spaces with thickness up to 5 mm.
notes: Both describe small bilateral pleural effusions. Minor measurement difference (4 vs 5 mm). Doctor was more specific about location (left > interlobar).

doctor_description: Pericardial effusion with asymmetric pericardial separation up to 6 mm over the left-sided chambers.
validator_description: Pericardial effusion with asymmetric pericardial separation up to 6 mm over the left-sided chambers.
notes: Concordant finding with identical description.

doctor_description: Mild coronary artery calcification.
validator_description: Moderate coronary artery calcification.
notes: Doctor graded as mild; validator graded as moderate. Severity grading difference, no management change.

validator_description: Fatty inclusions of the myocardium of the right heart.
notes: Validator identified fatty infiltration of the right ventricular myocardium not mentioned by the doctor. This can be a normal variant or may suggest arrhythmogenic right ventricular cardiomyopathy in appropriate clinical context. Worth documenting but typically does not change management on a CT chest.

doctor_description: Aortic wall calcification.
validator_description: Aortic calcification.
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: Main pulmonary artery is dilated to 2.8 cm. Findings suggestive of pulmonary hypertension.
validator_description: The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.
notes: Doctor reported MPA dilated at 2.8 cm and suggested pulmonary hypertension. Validator reported MPA as not dilated. 28 mm is borderline (normal upper limit ~29 mm). The validator's interpretation is more conservative. Given the borderline measurement, neither interpretation is definitively wrong.

doctor_description: Healed fractures of the left tenth and eleventh ribs.
validator_description: Healed fractures of the left tenth and eleventh ribs.
notes: Concordant finding.

validator_description: Hypodense lesion in the pedicle of T12 on the right side up to 1.5 cm (likely hemangioma).
notes: Validator identified a 1.5 cm hypodense lesion in the T12 pedicle, characterized as likely hemangioma. Doctor missed this finding. While likely benign, a 1.5 cm vertebral lesion should be documented. Validator characterized it confidently as hemangioma, so no additional workup recommended.

validator_description: In the fourth rib on the right low-density areas of osteosclerosis up to 1 cm.
notes: Validator identified osteosclerotic areas in the right 4th rib not mentioned by the doctor. In the setting of pulmonary nodules, bone lesions should be documented. However, described as low-density osteosclerosis, likely benign.

doctor_description: Moderate degenerative changes including osteochondrosis and spondylosis.
validator_description: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: Mild thickening of the medial crus of the left adrenal gland.
validator_description: Mild thickening of the medial crus of the left adrenal gland up to 9 mm, no follow-up is needed.
notes: Validator added a measurement (9 mm) and explicitly stated no follow-up needed. Same finding.

doctor_description: Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, follow-up CT in 3-6 months, then CT at 18-24 months.
validator_description: Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, follow-up CT in 3-6 months, then CT at 18-24 months.
notes: Concordant recommendation for nodule follow-up.

doctor_description: Interstitial thickening and patchy heterogeneous aeration, likely cardiogenic.
validator_description: May be cardiogenic, as well as manifestations of hypersensitivity pneumonitis.
notes: Validator added hypersensitivity pneumonitis to the differential in addition to cardiogenic cause. Doctor only mentioned cardiogenic etiology. Broadening the differential is clinically appropriate given the imaging pattern.

doctor_description: No nodules requiring follow-up are identified
validator_description: A few micronodules in the lower lobes of both lungs
notes: Validator identified micronodules that doctor missed. Validator added Fleischner Society follow-up recommendation in impression, though noted it may not be applicable given clinical history.

doctor_description: Senescent changes are present in the lungs
validator_description: Interstitial changes in the lungs - ground glass and reticulations, clinically insignificant
notes: Doctor called these 'senescent changes'; validator described them more specifically as ground glass and reticulations but still noted clinically insignificant. Different terminology for the same finding.

validator_description: Small hiatal hernia
notes: Doctor missed small hiatal hernia. Minor incidental finding, no management change.

doctor_description: The thyroid gland is heterogeneous, with microcalcifications in the parenchyma of the left lobe. The left thyroid lobe is enlarged, measuring 3.6 x 4.1 cm
validator_description: Both lobes are heterogeneous, the left thyroid lobe is enlarged, measuring 3.6 x 4.1 cm, with microcalcifications
notes: Validator noted both lobes heterogeneous (vs doctor noting only left lobe microcalcifications). Validator added ACR White Paper citation for thyroid ultrasound recommendation. Same clinical recommendation.

doctor_description: The main pulmonary artery is not dilated
validator_description: The main pulmonary artery is dilated (3.1 cm)
notes: Doctor stated main PA is not dilated; validator measured it at 3.1 cm and called it dilated with suggestion of pulmonary hypertension. Borderline measurement (upper limit of normal ~2.9 cm). Clinically relevant observation but unlikely to change immediate management.

validator_description: Osteosclerotic lesion of the vertebral body T11 up to 5 mm
notes: Doctor missed a small 5 mm osteosclerotic lesion in T11. Likely a bone island but worth mentioning, especially in an oncology patient. No immediate management change.

doctor_description: Mild atherosclerotic calcification of the coronary arteries and thoracic aorta
validator_description: Mild calcification of the coronary arteries
notes: Concordant finding.

doctor_description: Cardiothoracic ratio is 54%
validator_description: Cardiothoracic ratio is 0.54
notes: Same measurement, different notation (54% vs 0.54).

validator_description: Round gallstones, measuring up to 2.0 cm
notes: Concordant. Validator simplified the description slightly.

doctor_description: Severe degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with diffuse calcification of the anterior longitudinal ligament
validator_description: Severe degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with diffuse calcification of the anterior longitudinal ligament
notes: Concordant. Both describe DISH / Forestier disease.

doctor_description: Mild atherosclerotic calcification of the coronary arteries and thoracic aorta
validator_description: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with mild calcification
notes: Concordant.

doctor_description: In segment 2 of the right upper lobe, there is a subpleural solid pulmonary nodule measuring 5 mm (series 5, image 52). Decreased in size compared with December 05, 2022
validator_description: In the right upper lobe there is a subpleural solid pulmonary nodule measuring 5 mm (series 5, image 52). Decreased in size compared with December 05, 2022
notes: Concordant. Validator dropped the specific segment number but same finding.

doctor_description: The main pulmonary artery is dilated to 3.1 cm (previously 3.3 cm)
validator_description: The main pulmonary artery is dilated to 3.3 cm
notes: Doctor measured 3.1 cm and noted decrease from prior 3.3 cm. Validator measured 3.3 cm (same as prior). Minor measurement discrepancy. Both report dilation and suggest pulmonary hypertension.

doctor_description: Aortic valve calcification
validator_description: Aortic valve calcification
notes: Concordant.

doctor_description: Moderate coronary artery calcification
validator_description: Moderate coronary artery calcification
notes: Concordant.

doctor_description: Status post bilateral shoulder arthroplasty

Validator identified the nodule as slightly decreased. The difference in size description is clinically relevant as it affects Fleischner risk stratification, though both recommend continued follow-up. Overmeasurement of solid component could overestimate malignancy risk.

validator_description: Single calcifications are present in both lungs.

validator_description: Aorta not dilated. Pulmonary trunk not dilated.

doctor_description: Round hypodense (+4 HU) lesion up to 4 mm, unchanged.

doctor_description: Consistent with bronchiolitis. Findings can be consistent with infectious process (differentiate vs mycobacterial infection).
validator_description: Consistent with sequelae of bronchiolitis, without interval change.
notes: Doctor raised concern for active infectious process including mycobacterial infection. Validator characterized as sequelae (i.e., chronic/resolved). Given stability, validator's interpretation is more appropriate. No management change as both document the finding.

anatomical_location: Right middle lobe, left lower lobe, left upper lobe
doctor_description: Deformity and dilatation of most subsegmental bronchi with cylindrical bronchiectasis, more pronounced in the right middle lobe (with decreased RML volume) and left lower lobe.
validator_description: Cylindrical bronchiectasis of segmental and subsegmental bronchi in the right middle lobe, with decreased RML volume (partial atelectasis). Cylindrical bronchiectasis in the left lower lobe and left upper lobe.
notes: Both describe bronchiectasis in similar locations. Validator added left upper lobe involvement explicitly and characterized RML volume loss as partial atelectasis. Substantively concordant.

doctor_description: Multiple peribronchial subsolid nodules 2-5 mm forming a tree-in-bud pattern (more pronounced in RLL and LUL).
validator_description: Multiple centrilobular tree-in-bud nodules and bronchocele in both lungs.
notes: Both describe tree-in-bud pattern. Doctor called them 'peribronchial subsolid nodules,' validator used 'centrilobular tree-in-bud nodules.' Validator explicitly added bronchocele. Substantively equivalent description.

doctor_description: Solitary solid nodules in the left lung: upper lobe 1.0 x 8 mm (image 81) and upper lobe 5 mm along the minor interlobar fissure (image 106).
validator_description: Not mentioned separately.
notes: Doctor identified two separate solid nodules in the left upper lobe and recommended Fleischner 3-month follow-up. Validator did not mention these as separate findings, likely interpreting them as part of the diffuse centrilobular/tree-in-bud pattern rather than independent solid nodules. This is a clinically relevant interpretive difference -- if these are truly solid nodules separate from the infectious process, they warrant independent follow-up. However, in the context of diffuse airways disease, the validator's approach of not applying Fleischner to centrilobular nodules is correct per guidelines.

doctor_description: Not mentioned.
validator_description: Segmental and subsegmental bronchi of both lungs with secretion.
notes: Validator added bronchial secretion as supporting evidence of active airways disease. Not mentioned by doctor. Minor clinical addition, no management change.

doctor_description: Areas of linear and focal pulmonary fibrosis with formation of traction bronchiectasis perifissural bilaterally.
validator_description: Not mentioned separately.
notes: Doctor described perifissural traction bronchiectasis separately. Validator likely incorporated this into the overall bronchiectasis/airways disease description. Stylistic difference in organization rather than a true overreport.

doctor_description: The trachea and central bronchi are patent. The bronchial walls are thickened.
validator_description: Not mentioned separately (airways disease described differently).
notes: Doctor mentioned bronchial wall thickening as a separate finding. Validator subsumed this into the overall airways disease description. Organizational difference.

doctor_description: Differentiate residual postinflammatory changes vs chronic inflammatory changes including nontuberculous mycobacterial infection. Applied Fleischner 3-6 month follow-up.
validator_description: Findings consistent with non-tuberculous mycobacterial infection (Lady Windermere syndrome) with associated middle lobe syndrome. Fleischner Society recommendations cannot be applied to centrilobular nodules.
notes: Both mention NTM. The validator was more definitive in the diagnosis (Lady Windermere syndrome) and correctly noted that Fleischner does not apply to centrilobular nodules. Doctor incorrectly applied Fleischner 3-6 month follow-up to centrilobular nodules, which is a guideline application error. However, the clinical direction (follow-up imaging) is similar, so management impact is minimal.

doctor_description: Mild coronary artery calcification.
validator_description: Mild coronary artery calcification.

doctor_description: Not dilated. Wall calcification.

validator_description: Mild degenerative changes. Decreased attenuation at T12 and L2 to 140 HU, L1 to 133 HU (osteopenia).
notes: Concordant.

doctor_description: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.
validator_description: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.
notes: Concordant.

doctor_description: No nodules requiring follow-up are identified.
validator_description: Few solid nodules in both lungs: RML 4 mm, RLL 2 mm, RLL 3 mm, LUL 2 mm.
notes: Doctor stated no nodules requiring follow-up. Validator identified four solid nodules up to 4 mm. Per Fleischner, these small nodules likely do not require follow-up in low-risk patients, so doctor's conclusion about management was not incorrect. However, failing to describe the nodules themselves is an underreport -- they should be documented even if no follow-up is recommended.

doctor_description: Subsegmental atelectasis in the right middle lobe.
validator_description: Subsegmental atelectasis in the right middle lobe.
notes: Concordant.

doctor_description: Study performed with hypoventilatory changes in the posterior basal lung regions.
validator_description: Not mentioned.
notes: Doctor noted hypoventilatory changes as a technical observation. Validator omitted this. This is a common incidental observation related to breath-hold technique, not a true finding. Stylistic difference.

doctor_description: Not mentioned.
validator_description: On the right, the interlobar pleura is minimally thickened, possibly due to tiny amount of fluid.
notes: Validator noted minimal interlobar pleural thickening/fluid. This is a very minor observation with no clinical significance. Stylistic level.

doctor_description: Fat of the upper mediastinum is diffusely increased in attenuation (due to residual thymic tissue?), otherwise no visible masses.
validator_description: Residual thymic tissue in the prevascular space, no masses.
notes: Both describe the same finding. Doctor was uncertain and recommended 6-12 month follow-up CT in impression. Validator simply labeled it as residual thymus without recommending follow-up, which is more appropriate for a benign entity.

doctor_description: Consider follow-up CT in 6 to 12 months for diffuse increased attenuation of mediastinal fat (residual thymic tissue).
validator_description: Not recommended. Residual thymic tissue identified without follow-up suggestion.
notes: Doctor recommended unnecessary follow-up for what is likely normal residual thymic tissue. Validator correctly did not recommend follow-up. This overcall could lead to unnecessary imaging, though the clinical significance is low.

validator_description: Osteoblastic lesion in the T10 vertebral body and left pedicle with irregular margins 2.1 x 1.8 x 0.9 cm, +1350 HU.
notes: Validator specified extension into the left pedicle, which the doctor omitted. Both describe the same lesion with the same measurements. The pedicle involvement is relevant for characterization (bone island vs metastasis) but does not change the recommendation to correlate clinically.

doctor_description: Not mentioned.

notes: Doctor missed two small osteoblastic lesions in T7. In the context of the T10 osteoblastic lesion and possible oncologic history, additional bone lesions are relevant for staging and differential diagnosis (multiple bone islands vs metastases).

doctor_description: Moderate degenerative changes with subchondral sclerosis and osteophytes.
validator_description: Moderate degenerative changes with subchondral sclerosis and osteophytes.

validator_description: Hypodense fluid-attenuation lesion subcapsular in segment 2, up to 1.0 x 0.7 cm, sharp smooth margins. Punctate calcifications along capsule at segment 4.

notes: Validator identified a second small thyroid lesion (4 mm in lower pole). Both recommend thyroid ultrasound (validator deferred to ACR White Paper for <1.5 cm saying no follow-up needed). The additional 4 mm lesion does not change management.

doctor_description: Mass measuring 1.5 x 1.5 cm, sharp smooth margins.
validator_description: Solid mass measuring 2.0 x 1.5 x 1.5 cm, sharp irregular margins, anterior to the pectoral muscle.
notes: Validator measured the mass larger (2.0 cm vs 1.5 cm in one dimension), described it as solid with irregular margins (doctor said smooth margins), and specified its relationship to the pectoral muscle. Irregular margins are more concerning. Both recommend correlation with dedicated breast imaging, so management is the same, but the characterization difference is clinically meaningful.

doctor_description: Numerous solid nodules, stable. Largest: RUL 6 mm, RML 1.0x0.7 cm, LUL 5 mm, LLL 8 mm and 7 mm.
validator_description: Multiple solid nodules, stable. Same nodules listed with same sizes.
notes: Concordant. Same nodules, same measurements, same stability assessment.

validator_description: Reticular changes, ground glass opacities and a few bronchioloectasis in subpleural portions, more pronounced in RML and LLL, without interval change.
notes: Validator added ground glass opacities and bronchioloectasis to the subpleural changes, which are features of interstitial lung abnormalities. This is clinically relevant as the validator explicitly raised ILA in the impression with a Fleischner Society reference. Doctor did not mention this entity.

doctor_description: Not mentioned as ILA.

notes: Validator recognized the pattern of subpleural reticulation with GGO and bronchioloectasis as possible ILA and cited the Fleischner ILA position paper. Doctor did not raise this diagnosis. This is an important observation for patient management and prognosis, though no specific treatment change results from this CT alone.

doctor_description: Area of consolidation of nonmeasurable size, irregular shape with bandlike margins, involving perihilar portions of the right lung along the suture material. No significant bronchial narrowing.
validator_description: Spiculated central mass 5.6 x 3.2 x 6.0 cm, spreading into the middle and lower lobes, the lumen of the bronchial stump, with probable invasion of the mediastinum. Stump wall thickening. Consolidation in RML with smooth edges 5.0 x 2.3 cm.
notes: This is a significant underreport. Doctor described the mass as 'nonmeasurable consolidation' and considered postoperative changes as a possibility. Validator identified a measurable spiculated mass (5.6 x 3.2 x 6.0 cm) with probable mediastinal invasion and a separate consolidation in the RML. The validator's description strongly favors recurrence. The doctor was equivocal ('suspicious for recurrence; postoperative changes are less likely') whereas the validator was definitive ('recurrence of a lung neoplasm... with probable mediastinal invasion'). Measuring the mass and identifying mediastinal invasion are critical for staging and treatment planning.

validator_description: Thoracic aorta dilated in ascending portion up to 4.0 cm, with wall calcification.

notes: Validator characterized the sclerotic changes as osteoblastic lesions (implying possible metastases in context of lung cancer) and raised the possibility of pathological fractures. Doctor described 'sclerotic bone remodeling' more vaguely and described pseudoarthrosis without raising concern for pathological etiology. In a lung cancer patient, the validator's characterization is more appropriate and clinically relevant.

doctor_description: Aorta not dilated, with moderate wall calcification.
validator_description: Aorta not dilated. Mild wall calcification of thoracic aorta and brachiocephalic trunk.

A 6 mm simple cyst is indeed clinically insignificant and per ACR White Paper needs no follow-up, so the validator's decision to omit it is reasonable. Doctor was not wrong to mention it but added unnecessary detail that both agree requires no follow-up.

doctor_description: Solid nodule 5 mm, no change in morphology or size.
